title: Network Governance
summary: A lesson on the rules, decision-making surfaces, and coordination expectations that keep technical systems legible over time.

bodySections.title: Governance Surface
bodySections.body:
Governance is the set of mechanisms that decides how the system changes. That includes both formal rules and the practical coordination patterns people rely on when technical priorities conflict.

In a protocol setting, governance is inseparable from security because system evolution itself can introduce risk.

bodySections.title: Why Learners Should Care
bodySections.body:
Learners should care because architecture does not remain static. If you want to understand a network, you need to understand not only how it works today, but how it is allowed to change tomorrow.

That is why governance belongs beside cryptography in the curriculum.

quizQuestions.prompt: How does this lesson define governance?
Why is governance presented as a security concern?
choices: Because system changes can introduce new risk
Because governance replaces all technical controls
Because governance is only a legal issue
